children.heading: Average crop revenue election payment
children.label: 1 Average crop revenue election payment
children.text: The term “average crop revenue election payment” means a payment made to producers on a farm under section 8715 of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “base acres”, with respect to a covered commodity on a farm, means the number of acres established under as in effect on , subject to any adjustment under .section 7911 of this titlesection 8711 of this titleSeptember 30, 2007

children.heading: Peanuts
children.label: B Peanuts
children.text: The term “base acres for peanuts” has the meaning given the term in .section 8751 of this title

children.heading: Counter-cyclical payment
children.label: 3 Counter-cyclical payment
children.text: The term “counter-cyclical payment” means a payment made to producers on a farm under section 8714 of this title.1

children.heading: Covered commodity
children.label: 4 Covered commodity
children.text: The term “covered commodity” means wheat, corn, grain sorghum, barley, oats, upland cotton, long grain rice, medium grain rice, pulse crops, soybeans, and other oilseeds.

children.heading: Direct payment
children.label: 5 Direct payment
children.text: The term “direct payment” means a payment made to producers on a farm under section 8713 of this title.1

children.heading: Effective price
children.label: 6 Effective price
children.text: The term “effective price”, with respect to a covered commodity for a crop year, means the price calculated by the Secretary under section 8714 of this title to determine whether counter-cyclical payments are required to be made for that crop year.1

children.label: A
children.text: is produced from pure strain varieties of the Barbadense species or any hybrid of the species, or other similar types of extra long staple cotton, designated by the Secretary, having characteristics needed for various end uses for which United States upland cotton is not suitable and grown in irrigated cotton-growing regions of the United States designated by the Secretary or other areas designated by the Secretary as suitable for the production of the varieties or types; and

children.label: B
children.text: is ginned on a roller-type gin or, if authorized by the Secretary, ginned on another type gin for experimental purposes.

children.heading: Loan commodity
children.label: 8 Loan commodity
children.text: The term “loan commodity” means wheat, corn, grain sorghum, barley, oats, upland cotton, extra long staple cotton, long grain rice, medium grain rice, soybeans, other oilseeds, graded wool, nongraded wool, mohair, honey, dry peas, lentils, small chickpeas, and large chickpeas.

children.heading: Medium grain rice
children.label: 9 Medium grain rice
children.text: The term “medium grain rice” includes short grain rice.

children.heading: Other oilseed
children.label: 10 Other oilseed
children.text: The term “other oilseed” means a crop of sunflower seed, rapeseed, canola, safflower, flaxseed, mustard seed, crambe, sesame seed, or any oilseed designated by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (B), 85 percent of the base acres of a covered commodity on a farm on which direct payments or counter-cyclical payments are made; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of direct payments for each of the 2009 through 2011 crop years, 83.3 percent of the base acres for the covered commodity on a farm on which direct payments are made.

children.heading: Payment yield
children.label: 12 Payment yield
children.text: The term “payment yield” means the yield established for direct payments and the yield established for counter-cyclical payments under as in effect on , or under , for a farm for a covered commodity.section 7912 of this titlesection 8712 of this titleSeptember 30, 2007

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “producer” means an owner, operator, landlord, tenant, or sharecropper that shares in the risk of producing a crop and is entitled to share in the crop available for marketing from the farm, or would have shared had the crop been produced.

children.label: i
children.text: not take into consideration the existence of a hybrid seed contract; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure that program requirements do not adversely affect the ability of the grower to receive a payment under this chapter.

children.heading: Pulse crop
children.label: 14 Pulse crop
children.text: The term “pulse crop” means dry peas, lentils, small chickpeas, and large chickpeas.

children.label: A
children.text: a State;

children.label: B
children.text: the District of Columbia;

children.label: C
children.text: the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: Target price
children.label: 16 Target price
children.text: The term “target price” means the price per bushel, pound, or hundredweight (or other appropriate unit) of a covered commodity used to determine the payment rate for counter-cyclical payments.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 17 United States
children.text: The term “United States”, when used in a geographical sense, means all of the States.

children.heading: United States Premium Factor
children.label: 18 United States Premium Factor
children.text: The term “United States Premium Factor” means the percentage by which the difference in the United States loan schedule premiums for Strict Middling (SM) 1⅛-inch upland cotton and for Middling (M) 1⁄-inch upland cotton exceeds the difference in the applicable premiums for comparable international qualities.332
